trigger_reason: The reviewed system has repeated scheduled workflows, locks, idempotency-sensitive exchange hashes, organization import/main movement, and update side effects.

commands.summary: Correct model failed on update_apply_gate after failed status retained update_available and user_requested; broken variant failed as expected on duplicate upload side effects.
Production replay passed lock exclusivity, organization main-only download, and prepared-plus-UI-closed update gate scenarios.

commands.command: python -m unittest tests.test_maintenance_lanes tests.test_org_sources tests.test_software_update tests.test_org_automation
commands.summary: 26 focused tests passed.

findings: Failed update state can be retried automatically by Architect without a fresh explicit user prepare action if update_available and user_requested remain true.
Maintenance lane runners release locks on normal return paths but do not wrap the whole stateful run in try/finally, so unexpected exceptions rely on stale-lock recovery.
counterexamples: architect_update_check -> update_failed -> architect_update_check can apply again from failed state when update_available=true and user_requested=true.
skipped_steps: formal flowguard package run skipped: Python module flowguard is not installed in this workspace
friction_points: The fallback model is executable but not a formal flowguard package run.
next_actions: Decide whether failed updates should require a fresh user prepare action before retry.
Consider wrapping stateful maintenance lane bodies in finally release/status handling.

task_summary: Implement failed-update retry gate and finally-based maintenance lock release
trigger_reason: The prior model-first review exposed an automatic retry path after failed updates and stale-lock dependence in long maintenance lanes.

commands.summary: Correct model passed 55770 explored paths; intentionally broken duplicate-upload variant still failed as expected.
Production conformance replay passed update retry gating, prepared apply gating, lock exclusivity, lock failure cleanup, and organization main-only download scenarios.

commands.command: python -m unittest tests.test_software_update tests.test_maintenance_lanes tests.test_org_automation tests.test_kb_dream tests.test_kb_architect
commands.summary: 44 focused tests passed.
218 tests passed.
Desktop KB check passed with 138 entries.
Install health checklist passed.
No whitespace errors; PowerShell reported expected CRLF normalization warnings for touched files.

findings: Implemented failed update as a terminal waiting state for the same target by clearing user_requested and preventing Architect from retrying until the user prepares again.
A newly discovered remote target after a failed update now moves back to available and still requires fresh user preparation before upgrading.
Dream, Architect, organization contribution, and organization maintenance now write failed lane status and release their lane lock from a finally path on unexpected exceptions.
counterexamples: Resolved previous failed-update auto-retry trace: failed same target now emits update_failed_awaiting_user instead of upgrading.
skipped_steps: formal flowguard package run skipped: Python module flowguard is not installed in this workspace; user requested not to address that tooling gap now
friction_points: A first mechanical attempt to wrap large maintenance functions matched the first return instead of the full function boundary; py_compile caught it, and the implementation was redone using function-boundary-aware wrapping.
next_actions: Keep the formal flowguard package/toolchain gap as a separate follow-up owned outside this fix.
Use function-boundary-aware edits for large stateful functions when adding outer try/finally guards.

task_summary: Model card creation surfaces and zh-CN display translation cleanup
trigger_reason: The user asked whether all cards should be created only by Sleep and whether missing Chinese display text indicates the Sleep i18n cleanup did not run.

commands.summary: flowguard schema version 1.0 is importable in this workspace.
Strict Sleep-only creation failed as expected; ideal Sleep with i18n cleanup passed; observed workflow includes paths that leave missing zh-CN display fields.
Model file compiled.
9 i18n tests passed.

findings: The current system has legitimate non-Sleep card creation surfaces: manual candidate capture, Dream candidate creation, and organization adoption.
Sleep candidate creation plus an applied i18n cleanup closes the zh-CN display gap in the model.
A Sleep pass that creates or sees cards but finalizes without an applied i18n plan can leave English-only cards visible in the UI until a later i18n pass.
counterexamples: card_created_by_manual -> not_sleep_i18n_owner -> active_task_done violates the strict all-cards-created-by-Sleep hypothesis.
card_created_by_sleep -> sleep_i18n_cleanup_skipped -> sleep_finalized_with_missing_i18n violates the Sleep-finalization-without-missing-i18n invariant.
skipped_steps: production conformance replay skipped: this task was read-only diagnosis and did not change production card/i18n code.
friction_points: ScenarioRun summaries expose ok as absent/null, so the model uses compact label summaries for exact scenario traces.
next_actions: Treat missing zh-CN on current cards as a Sleep i18n follow-up gap unless a direct card creation path intentionally wrote the card after the last cleanup.
Consider making Sleep finalization or reporting fail loudly when review-i18n actions remain after candidate creation or semantic text changes.

findings: No immediate production regression was found in tests, install health, desktop payload checks, or existing conformance replay.

The main stateful architecture model is stale relative to the updated skill: flowguard is now importable, but .flowguard/khaos_brain_function_flow.py still uses a custom standard-library explorer and reports flowguard_package_available=false.
The i18n model still shows a future Sleep workflow risk: a Sleep pass can finalize after card creation or semantic text changes without applying the zh-CN cleanup plan.
Current card text i18n is clean, but 16 route segments still rely on English fallback in zh-CN display labels.
friction_points: The updated skill correctly requires real flowguard import preflight, but the older Khaos main model still contains stale fallback wording and metadata from before flowguard was installed.

The skill is very broad for read-only inspection: it says to start an in_progress adoption log before modeling, but real review often discovers whether a new model/log is needed only after inspecting existing local models.
Scenario exact-sequence reports expose ok as null in some compact summaries, so reporting has to rely on labels unless the skill or helper offers a normalized scenario status wrapper.
next_actions: Discuss whether to migrate .flowguard/khaos_brain_function_flow.py from the custom fallback explorer to the real flowguard Workflow/Explorer API now that schema 1.0 is available.
Consider making Sleep completion mark the run incomplete or failed-loudly when review-i18n actions remain after candidate creation or semantic text changes.
Add zh-CN display labels for the 16 missing route segments or leave them as a normal proposal-only maintenance target.

Plan simulation model compiled.

commands.command: python -m unittest tests.test_kb_i18n tests.test_kb_architect tests.test_codex_install tests.test_kb_consolidate_apply_worker1
commands.summary: 36 focused tests passed.
No whitespace errors; Git reported existing CRLF-normalization warnings for flowguard logs.

counterexamples: sleep_content_change -> sleep_finish is blocked as sleep_finish_blocked_missing_i18n.
findings: The planned flow is internally consistent when Sleep owns one final AI zh-CN cleanup pass for both card text and route display text.
The old separate translation step should be disabled rather than duplicated; the model rejects a variant where legacy i18n still applies translations mid-run.
Architect can safely own the system-readable maintenance rollup if it refuses to mark the rollup complete until Sleep, Dream, FlowGuard, organization, and install reports are present.
Release/update readiness should remain blocked until content boundaries are reviewed and repository-managed skill changes have been installed and checked.
friction_points: Long complete workflows should be verified with exact sequences; bounded exhaustive exploration should only require labels reachable in short paths to avoid false reachability failures.
next_actions: Implement the plan in small batches: Sleep final cleanup first, then Architect rollup, then content-boundary/install-sync hardening.
Keep the new plan model as a regression guard while implementing the production changes.
skipped_steps: No production code was changed; this task was simulation-only before implementation.
